oneLiner: A native macOS terminal emulator (SwiftUI + embedded Ghostty) with a workspace model and CLI built for driving Claude Code sessions.
next: Validate harness v1.1.0 on a real multi-slice run: confirm async fan-out (`--no-wait` + `implement-wait.sh`) yields multi-branch overlap and that the STOP block/structured handoff flips the orchestrator output/resident inversion (ADR 0003); re-audit with session-audit.

milestones.title: Agent delegation (/tian implement) shipped in a release

shipped.summary: Sidebar per-tab worktree branch rows: each Claude-task tab shows its own branch + diff/PR badges (git status keyed by worktree root, not the shared --git-common-dir), a Claude->tian worktree bridge (`tian pane set-directory` + CwdChanged/PostToolUse hooks sync each pane's Claude cwd so EnterWorktree reflects in the sidebar), and an active-tab indicator. 874 unit tests green.

Fixed worktree/child Spaces showing the parent repo's branch/status in the sidebar on restore — a restored parent-repo pane clobbered the worktree's shared GitRepoID (worktree + parent share one id, keyed on --git-common-dir). Anchored git status to the worktree path and guarded the worktree repo from pane-GC unpin. Ran via /orchestrate (2 waves), /code-review-hardened; full unit suite 861 green.

/tian implement harness v1.1.0: async fan-out (`implement.sh --no-wait` + `implement-wait.sh` await-by-log), orchestrator-leanness STOP block + `files:` handoff in the self-verify report, and run-log parent↔child linkage so session-audit links children authoritatively + filters zombies. Self-verified, independently verified, and /code-review --fix found+fixed 7 real defects. Driven by a session audit of real runs.
